summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacon scripts that exfiltrate host info over HTTP
details: On install, package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node run.js") that executes bundled beacon scripts. beacon27.js imports child_process and https, reads process.env and process.platform, and issues outbound GET requests carrying that data. beacon_linux.js imports os, http, and child_process, gathers os.hostname(), os.platform(), and shell-exec output, and POSTs to a hardcoded http.request destination. The package's only documented behavior on install is to harvest installer host identifiers and environment data and beacon them outward — there is no legitimate functionality that requires this on a metrics-pipeline scaffold. The package name and structure (random suffix, single release, no library API) are consistent with a disposable exfiltration dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
